_comment: Sample project config. The 'add this project' skill writes one of these for you from inside a repo; this is the manual reference. 'owner' is the GitHub org/user — the worker uses it to auto-clone on the first handoff. 'roots' are the paths that hold the repo(s) on each machine: for a SINGLE repo, point roots at the repo itself (the worker clones owner/<basename> there). For a POLYREPO, point roots at the parent dir and add a 'repos' list of member names (each cloned under the parent, minus never_mirror). The soft denied-command guardrail is now GLOBAL (/opt/baton/denied.json), not per-project.
name: example
